name: Si Province
_note: 司隸州 — Capital district around Luoyang (east) and Chang'an (west), narrow E-W corridor across central Henan and Shaanxi.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, drawn from training-data knowledge of Han geography rather than CHGIS data. Covers the corridor between the two capitals (Luoyang in the east, Chang'an in the west); not CHGIS-precise and may need refinement.

name: Yu Province
_note: 豫州 — central Henan + western Anhui, centered on Xuchang and Runan.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers central Henan around Xuchang and Runan; not boundary-precise.

name: Yan Province
_note: 兗州 — western Shandong + eastern Henan, centered on Dongjun/Puyang.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers western Shandong and the Henan/Shandong border; not boundary-precise.

name: Qing Province
_note: 青州 — Shandong peninsula east of Yan Province.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers the Shandong peninsula east of modern Jinan; not boundary-precise.

name: Xu Province
_note: 徐州 — northern Jiangsu + parts of Anhui and southern Shandong, centered on Pengcheng (modern Xuzhou).
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers northern Jiangsu around Xuzhou with a wedge into southern Shandong; not boundary-precise.

name: Ji Province
_note: 冀州 — southern Hebei + northern Henan, centered on Yejun (modern Linzhang).
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers southern Hebei around Ye and Handan; not boundary-precise.

name: You Province
_note: 幽州 — northeastern frontier: northern Hebei, Beijing, Liaoning west.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers the northeastern frontier from northern Hebei through Liaoning's west; not boundary-precise.

name: Bing Province
_note: 并州 — modern Shanxi province + parts of Inner Mongolia, centered on Jinyang (modern Taiyuan).
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers modern Shanxi province; not boundary-precise.

name: Liang Province
_note: 凉州 — northwestern frontier: Gansu + parts of Ningxia and Qinghai east. Split off Yong Province in late Han.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers the Gansu corridor northwest of Chang'an; not boundary-precise.

name: Yong Province
_note: 雍州 — split off Liang around 213 AD: modern Shaanxi + parts of Gansu southeast. Overlaps Si Province's western half.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers Shaanxi and parts of southern Gansu; overlaps with Si and Liang because the historical division shifted; not boundary-precise.

name: Jing Province
_note: 荆州 — modern Hubei + Hunan, centered on Xiangyang and Jiangling. Liu Biao's territory; major theater for Red Cliffs.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers modern Hubei and Hunan; not boundary-precise.

name: Yang Province
_note: 扬州 — Wu's heartland: southern Jiangsu + Anhui + Zhejiang + Jiangxi + Fujian.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers modern Jiangsu south, Anhui, Zhejiang, Jiangxi, Fujian; not boundary-precise.

name: Yi Province
_note: 益州 — Shu Han's heartland: Sichuan basin + Chongqing + parts of Yunnan / Guizhou / southern Shaanxi.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers Sichuan basin and southern Shaanxi (Hanzhong); not boundary-precise.

name: Jiao Province
_note: 交州 — far south: Guangdong + Guangxi + northern Vietnam. Split into Jiao + Guang in 226 AD.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers the modern Guangdong/Guangxi/northern Vietnam region prior to its 226 split; not boundary-precise.

name: Guang Province
_note: 廣州 — created 226 AD from Jiao Province: modern Guangdong + eastern Guangxi.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers modern Guangdong post-226 split from Jiao; not boundary-precise.

name: Ning Province
_note: 寧州 — created late in the Three Kingdoms period (around the 270s) from southern Yi Province: modern Yunnan + parts of Guizhou.
notes_append: Map polygon: approximate Han-era extent, training-data sourced (not CHGIS). Covers modern Yunnan post-270s split from Yi; not boundary-precise.
